reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — real transformers.js@3.7.5 run in a Web Worker: fresh profile → Download (~110 MB, no auto-download) → auto-init ready → Classify on the cats sample returned Egyptian cat 55.2%, tabby 26.4%, tiger cat 6.5% (correct ImageNet labels), 514 ms WASM, 1000 classes. Pre-verified independently too: beit-base q8 via pipeline() on the same image → Egyptian cat 0.55, confirming the low-level processor→model path matches. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/w18bshots/beit-classification-{light,dark,mobile}.png
rubric.notes: DISTINCT from the built supervised ViT: BEiT is a SELF-SUPERVISED Vision Transformer (masked-image-modeling / BERT-for-images pretraining), same ViT-Base architecture + same ImageNet task. Note BEiT's top-1 (Egyptian cat) differs from ViT's (tabby) on this image — the head-to-head surfaces exactly that self-supervised-vs-supervised divergence.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — output.logits is [1,1000]; the worker computes softmax + top-k + entropy + top-1 margin. Semantic sanity holds: the cats photo yields cat classes ranked top (Egyptian cat 55.2%, tabby 26.4%, tiger cat 6.5%), the See-inside table shows the raw logit next to the softmax %, and the BEiT-vs-ViT head-to-head reports agreement/disagreement with each model's top-1. conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual (agent verdict recorded).
rubric.notes: 1000 classes, same label space as ViT — which is what makes the self-supervised-vs-supervised head-to-head directly comparable.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (Xenova/beit-base-patch16-224 linked to card), declares-task=pass (image-classification), declares-backend=pass (WebAssembly), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker via the shared classify-ui.js ClassifierEngine). At-a-glance also states params (~86M), 16×16 patches, the self-supervised MIM pretraining + ImageNet fine-tune, Apache-2.0.
rubric.notes: q8 (model_quantized.onnx, ~110 MB) verified NON-degenerate — returns correct cat labels, so q8 is the honest choice.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — fresh profile shows 'Download model (~110 MB)' and does NOT auto-download; clicking streams progress and the shared loader auto-inits to 'Model ready'. Cached reload (new tab, same profile) → state=ready with NO Download button (only 'Clear cached model'). All 4 sub-pages load clean from a fresh profile showing the honest Download state. conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass, auto-init-on-cached=manual.
rubric.notes: Verified across the state matrix: first visit (absent→Download, no silent large re-download of the 110 MB model) + current-cached (auto-init, no button).

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — streamed download progress; Retry surfaced by the shared loader on failure; classification errors go to a .status.err region ('Classification failed: …'); the head-to-head loads ViT lazily with its own progress line and honest ~88 MB note. Runs on the WASM path with no GPU (headless no-GPU produced real labels). conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass; honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback = manual.

Agent verdict — Classify button, top-k slider (live label), keyboard-operable dropzone + sample thumbnails (Enter/Space), 'Compare on this image' head-to-head button, the masked-image-modeling <details>, and per-sub-page controls (batch tag slider + tag-cloud filter chips; the wild patch-mask slider + re-roll + noise toggle; the ensemble button) all operable. conformance: controls-operable=manual.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified: the wild patch-mask canvas confirmed rendering 19968 grey masked px at 40% mask.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — every label comes from the on-device worker run (verified real output Egyptian cat 55.2% / tabby 26.4%). The head-to-head and ensemble reuse the same real ClassifierEngine driving the BEiT and ViT workers; no canned result anywhere. conformance: no-fake-output=manual.

conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. role=status aria-live loader + run status; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label; sample thumbnails keyboard-operable; chips have min-block-size 40px; the wild mask/noise controls are labelled native inputs.
rubric.notes: responsive-check flagged sub-24px targets (range-slider tracks / thumbnail height / checkbox); agent-reviewed as acceptable — actionable chips/buttons meet 40px and sliders/checkbox are native controls with adequate touch slop.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — desktop 1280×800 (light) + dark and mobile 360×740 DPR3 all render with no horizontal overflow (mobile measured 0px); the at-a-glance table and the two-column versus-grid wrap (grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px,1fr)) so BEiT/ViT stack on narrow screens); bar labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere; the wild control row uses flex-wrap. responsive-check programmatic pass on both classes; screenshots reviewed. conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass, parity-both-classes=manual.

Agent verdict — all inference (processor + model forward) runs in a Web Worker via the shared ClassifierEngine; the control UI (sliders, toggles) never blocks on inference; the head-to-head runs BEiT + ViT with Promise.all off-thread; the batch-tagging loop awaits each off-thread classify so it never blocks the main thread; the wild mask redraw is a cheap canvas op. Main-thread eval stayed responsive during the 514 ms WASM run; 0 console errors. conformance: inp-stays-low=manual.
rubric.notes: BEiT-Base is the heaviest of the classification demos (~110 MB, ~86M params) — still WASM-only and off-thread, so INP is unaffected; the download size is stated honestly in at-a-glance and the loader.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and two-column head-to-head layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing + flexible tracks (fr, minmax()) before fixed widths; use repeat(auto-fit, minmax()) for responsive card grids; min-inline-size:0 / overflow-wrap on items with long unbreakable content; flex-wrap on control rows.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the BEiT-vs-ViT panels use .versus-grid = grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px,1fr)) so they sit side-by-side on desktop and stack on mobile with no overflow; versus-col has min-inline-size:0; bar labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere; the wild page's mask/re-roll/noise control row uses flex-wrap; the run panel uses flex:1 1 280px.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; mobile screenshot measured 0px horizontal overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live status region loading progress and keyboard-operable controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; announce state in a role=status aria-live region; adequate tap targets; keyboard operability for custom controls.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + run status are role=status aria-live=polite; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow; sample thumbnails and dropzone handle Enter/Space; chips have min-block-size 40px; the wild mask slider, re-roll chip and noise checkbox are labelled native controls.

guidanceConsulted.query: batch process many images without blocking the main thread / keep INP low
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Yield to the main thread during long loops so input + rendering stay responsive; move heavy work off-thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference runs in a Web Worker (ClassifierEngine), so the batch-tagging loop awaits an off-thread classify per image and the main thread is never blocked; the head-to-head runs both workers concurrently with Promise.all.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0 console errors; UI responsive during the 514 ms WASM run and the batch loop

guidanceConsulted.query: keep inference off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy/background work off the main thread so the UI stays responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all classification runs in a Web Worker; the head-to-head runs the BEiT and ViT workers concurrently off-thread, keeping the control UI responsive.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0 console errors; main-thread eval stayed responsive during load

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css; the page links it.

openQuestions: Would visualising BEiT's actual masked-patch reconstruction (loading the pretraining head, not just the fine-tuned classifier) make the masked-image-modeling story even more legible, or is the 110 MB classifier the right honest scope?
Does the self-supervised BEiT genuinely tolerate patch masking better than the supervised ViT at matched mask rates — worth a side-by-side occlusion-robustness curve?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a BEiT-vs-ViT occlusion-robustness curve: sweep the mask rate on one image and plot both models' top-1 confidence, directly testing whether masked pretraining buys robustness to missing patches.
Add a label-efficiency framing demo: show BEiT's self-supervised features as a frozen backbone for few-shot fine-tuning on user images (the practical payoff of self-supervised pretraining).

summary: Revision-1 agent build+matrix pass for beit-classification: a NEW image-classification demo for a DISTINCT self-supervised Vision Transformer (masked-image-modeling / BERT-for-images pretraining) alongside the built SUPERVISED ViT — same ViT-Base architecture + same ImageNet task, different pretraining. Real ~110 MB download + on-device transformers.js q8 run verified in headless Chrome (cats sample → Egyptian cat 55.2%, tabby 26.4%, 514 ms WASM, 1000 classes); q8 confirmed non-degenerate. The BEiT-vs-ViT head-to-head loads ViT lazily and reports agreement/disagreement + per-model latency, making the self-supervised-vs-supervised contrast legible (they even split top-1 on the cats sample). Cached reload auto-inits with no Download button; all 4 sub-pages (basics / practical batch-tagging / wild patch-masking / multimodel self-vs-supervised ensemble) load clean, and the wild patch-mask canvas — thematically tied to BEiT's own pretraining game — renders correctly. Light+dark and mobile+desktop screenshots reviewed (no overflow, legible, ≥40px controls). 23 immutable conformance assertions authored; both gates green.
